description:
🇩🇪 Willkommen am livespotting Teststand in Melle, Niedersachsen.

Unsere Livecam am Futterhaus zeigt tagsüber verschiedene Singvögel in Nahaufnahme und wechselt zur Abenddämmerung in die Totale, um dämmerungs- und nachtaktive Wildtiere sichtbar zu machen.

Ein echter Hotspot für die lokale Tierwelt auf unserer Natur-Bühne. Hier testen wir Kameras verschiedener Hersteller unter realen Bedingungen. Die Kamera liefert ruhige Einblicke in Artenvielfalt rund um das Vogelhaus und die umliegende Wiese.

🐦 Tagsüber – Singvögel am Futterhaus
Zu sehen sind unter anderem:
– Finken: Grünfink, Buchfink, Kernbeißer
– Meisen: Kohlmeise, Blaumeise, Tannenmeise
– Kleiber
– Spechte: Buntspecht, Grünspecht, Kleinspecht
– Dompfaff
– Stieglitz
– Eichhörnchen und weitere Besucher

🌙 Abends & nachts – Wildtiere rund um das Gelände
Mit Beginn der Dämmerung zeigt die Totale gelegentlich:
– Rehe
– Damwild
– Hirsche
– Mufflons
– Eulen
– Wildschweine
– nachtaktive Kleintiere

🎥 Natur live erleben
Dieses Setup dient als Testumgebung für Wildtier- und Naturkameras. Die Livecam zeigt, wie sich unterschiedliche Modelle in echter Umgebung verhalten. Ein ruhiger Stream für Tierfreunde, Technikinteressierte und alle, die natürliche Abläufe beobachten möchten.

🇬🇧 Welcome to the livespotting test site in Melle, Lower Saxony.

Our live camera at the bird feeder shows various songbirds in close-up during the day and switches to a wide view at dusk to reveal wildlife active in the evening and at night.

This area is a hotspot for local wildlife. We use this location to test cameras from different manufacturers in real conditions. The livestream offers calm real-time views of bird activity and animals around the meadow.

🐦 During the day – Songbirds at the feeder
Species that may appear:
– Finches: European greenfinch, chaffinch, hawfinch
– Tits: great tit, blue tit, coal tit
– Nuthatch
– Woodpeckers: great spotted, green, lesser spotted
– Bullfinch
– European goldfinch
– Squirrels and more

🌙 At dusk & night – Wildlife around the area
Occasional sightings include:
– Roe deer
– Fallow deer
– Red deer
– Mouflon
– Owls
– Wild boar
– Nocturnal small animals

🎥 Nature in real time
This livestream is part of our testing setup for wildlife and nature cameras. It shows how different models perform outdoors. Suitable for bird lovers, wildlife fans and people interested in natural behaviour.

title: WATCH LIVE: NASA Artemis II Crew Now Farthest from Earth Ever in Historic Flight | APT
description:
Join this channel to get access to perks:
https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCpLEtz3H0jSfEneSdf1YKnw/join

Four astronauts aboard NASA’s Artemis II mission have reached a historic milestone—traveling farther from Earth than any humans before.

The crew, currently on a deep space journey around the Moon, has surpassed previous distance records set during the Apollo era. This marks a major moment in space exploration as humans venture deeper into space after more than 50 years.

The Artemis II mission is NASA’s first crewed mission of the Artemis program, testing the Orion spacecraft and paving the way for future lunar landings and eventual missions to Mars.

As the spacecraft continues its lunar flyby, astronauts are capturing unprecedented views of space and the Moon’s far side.

Watch LIVE for real-time updates, visuals, and key mission milestones. 

#ArtemisII #NASA #SpaceLive #LIVE

title: Earth live stream from ISS - 2 Cams | Tracker | Live Telemetry 04.06.2026. ​​
description:
Estação Espacial internacional e Terra ao vivo com rastreador e telemetria.
Enjoy splendid live views from the International Space Station (ISS) on two cameras simultaneously. The External High Definition Camera (EHDC) which most of the time is aimed directly at the Earth, and the other camera (Live) which usually shows the Station from the outside but also from the inside, with astronauts carrying out their daily activities.
The video also has a tracker to let you know where the ISS is, and also some real-time telemetry data, mainly the current attitude (yaw, pitch and roll).

Desfrute de esplêndidas vistas ao vivo da Estação Espacial Internacional (ISS) em duas câmeras simultaneamente. A Câmera Externa de Alta Definição (EHDC) que na maioria das vezes é voltada diretamente para a Terra, e a outra câmera (Ao Vivo) que geralmente mostra a Estação por fora, mas também por dentro, com os astronautas realizando suas atividades diárias. O vídeo também possui um rastreador para que você saiba onde está a ISS, e também alguns dados de telemetria em tempo real, principalmente a atitude atual (guinada, inclinação e rotação / yaw-pitch-roll).

title: NASA Artemis 2 LIVE Moon From Orbit
description:
LIVE NASA Artemis 2 Orion VIEW Moon Cam Watch live here!! Artemis II, NASA’s first crewed Moon mission in over 50 years, will launch astronauts around the Moon aboard the Orion spacecraft using the powerful SLS rocket. This historic NASA lunar mission will test deep-space life-support systems, high-speed Earth reentry, and the next phase of the Artemis program leading to the Artemis III Moon landing.
The Artemis 2 crew—Reid Wiseman, Victor Glover, Christina Koch, and Jeremy Hansen—will travel farther from Earth than any humans in decades, performing a lunar flyby, systems checkout, and deep-space trajectory critical for future Moon to Mars exploration. This mission is a major milestone for NASA Artemis, lunar exploration, Moon mission timeline, and the return of humans to the lunar surface. Space fans are watching for updates on Artemis II launch date, SLS rocket tests, Orion spacecraft performance, and NASA livestream coverage. Artemis II is the flagship NASA Artemis mission that will send the Artemis 2 crew of four Artemis II astronauts on a historic crewed lunar mission aboard the Orion spacecraft, launched by NASA’s powerful SLS rocket. This mission includes a precise lunar flyby and marks a major milestone in the Artemis program, advancing NASA’s long-term Moon to Mars strategy. As part of the modern NASA Moon mission campaign, Artemis II will test deep-space systems critical for future lunar exploration, including the planned Artemis 3 Moon landing. Space fans tracking the Moon mission timeline are watching closely for the official Artemis 2 launch date, major NASA spaceflight updates, and the upcoming NASA livestream. Expect major space news 2025 announcements as NASA releases each Moon mission update and continues preparing for humanity’s return—returning to the Moon for the first time in more than 50 years. Artemis Launch Live: Everything You Need to Know About NASA’s Return to the Moon
The Artemis launch live event marks one of the most anticipated moments in modern spaceflight, as NASA prepares for a historic rocket launch today live from Kennedy Space Center Florida. This mission to the moon 2026 is part of the broader Artemis program, designed to return humans to the Moon for the first time since Apollo 17, the last moon mission in 1972.

The Artemis 2 mission is NASA’s first crewed flight in the Artemis program, following the success of Artemis 1 launch. Unlike earlier missions, Artemis II will not land on the moon, but instead will send astronauts aboard the Orion capsule on a deep-space journey around the Moon.

title: Watch Live: NASA Artemis II Crew Sets Distance Record With Lunar Flyby
description:
We're back up LIVE - Artemis II crew is making their flight around the Moon!

NASA's Artemis II astronauts are on course to make history on Monday when they conduct a lunar flyby around the farside of the moon - a first for humans since Apollo 17 in 1972. The Orion spacecraft carrying the crew is on a course that will take them farther from Earth than any human has ever been, traveling 252,760 miles away, and breaking the Apollo 13's distance record of 248,655 miles set in 1970.

title: WATCH LIVE: NASA's Artemis Record-Breaking Lunar Flyby | Live Views from Orion Artemis II | N18G
description:
Artemis II Moon Rocket Lunch LIVE: NASA's Artemis II LIVE: Orion spacecraft on the Artemis II mission flies past the moon | Artemis II Astronauts | CNBC TV18
Live coverage as the Orion spacecraft on the Artemis II mission flies past the moon. SPACE - Artemis II mission expected to make its lunar flyby aboard the Orion spacecraft. Three U.S. astronauts and a Canadian have embarked on humanity's first crewed lunar voyage in more than half a century, a 10-day flight around the moon and back that will mark the second mission of Artemis, successor to the Apollo program of the Cold War era.
On Monday, April 6, the four astronauts of Artemis II will travel farther from Earth than any humans in history—breaking the record set by Apollo 13 in 1970. They'll sail around the far side of the Moon, photographing lunar features never before seen by human eyes. At their closest point, they'll pass roughly 4,000 miles above the lunar surface.
NASA released the very first images taken by astronauts aboard the Artemis II Orion capsule as they are making their way to the moon. The stunning pictures were taken by mission commander Reid Wiseman using his Personal Computing Device — a tablet that includes a camera — and they are both views of Earth.NASA Sends Astronauts around the Moon for the first time in 50 years.
LIVE feed provides continuous coverage of Artemis II mission activities with live commentary, beginning with tanking of the SLS (Space Launch System) rocket at NASA's Kennedy Space Center and concluding with the recovery of the Orion Integrity spacecraft after splashdown.
Live coverage of Artemis II's launch and lunar flyby, as well as daily mission briefings, will also stream on this feed. 
Live view of the Artemis II Moon rocket. 
Four astronauts — three from NASA and one from the CSA (Canadian Space Agency) — make up the Artemis II crew:
NASA astronaut Reid Wiseman, Artemis II commander
NASA astronaut Victor Glover, Artemis II pilot
NASA astronaut Christina Koch, Artemis II mission specialist
Canadian Space Agency (CSA) astronaut Jeremy Hansen, Artemis II mission specialist
After launching into space atop NASA's Space Launch System (SLS) rocket, the crew will journey around the Moon and back in their Orion spacecraft, named Integrity, on an approximately 10-day mission. Artemis II will be the first crewed flight test of SLS and Orion, testing the technologies we'll need for long-term lunar exploration and human missions to Mars.

title: NASA Artemis II Orion Spacecraft Lunar Flyby | LIVE
description:
Mission control teams in Houston and the Artemis II crew completed an outbound correction burn to refine the Orion spacecraft’s trajectory to the Moon. The burn began at 11:03 p.m. EDT and lasted 17.5 seconds.

NASA astronauts Reid Wiseman, Victor Glover, and Christina Koch, along with CSA (Canadian Space Agency) astronaut Jeremy Hansen, continue on a precise path to flyby the Moon on Monday, April 6.  

Early in their workday, the crew completed a key test objective of the mission: the Orion Crew Survival System (OCSS) suit.  

All four crew members conducted a full sequence of tests, including putting on and pressurizing the suit, performing leak checks, simulating seat entry, and assessing mobility and their ability to eat and drink. The suit protects astronauts during dynamic phases of flight and provides life support in the event of cabin depressurization and survival operations after splashdown. 

Wrapping up the day, the crew will enter the Moon’s sphere of influence at 12:41 a.m. Monday, April 6, when the Moon’s gravity becomes the dominant force controlling Orion’s trajectory.  

That milestone sets the stage for the main event of flight day 6: the crew’s flight around the far side of the Moon. 

Watch live coverage of the Artemis II lunar flyby on NASA+, Amazon Prime, Apple TV, Hulu, Netflix, HBO Max and Roku beginning at 1 p.m., alongside the agency’s 24/7 coverage on its YouTube channel. Learn how to stream NASA content through a variety of online platforms, including social media.  

Key lunar flyby times, milestones (all times Eastern subject to change based on real-time operations): 

1 p.m.: NASA+ coverage of lunar flyby begins. 
1:56 p.m.: The crew will surpass the record for humans’ farthest distance from Earth, previously set by Apollo 13 in 1970. 
2:10 p.m.: Crew remarks on breaking Apollo 13 distance record (audio only) 
2:15 p.m.: Crew configures Orion’s cabin for flyby operations 
2:45 p.m.: Lunar observation begins 
6:44 p.m.: Predicted loss of communications as crew heads behind the Moon (approximately 40 minutes) 
7:02 p.m. Orion closest approach to the Moon (4,070 miles)
7:07 p.m.: Orion reaches maximum distance from Earth (252,757 miles) 
7:25 p.m.: “Earthrise” marks Earth coming back into view on the opposite edge of the moon; Predicted acquisition of communications as crew reemerges from behind the Moon
8:35-9:32 p.m.: During a solar eclipse, the Sun will pass behind the Moon from the crew’s perspective
9:20 p.m.: Lunar observation concludes 
The crew is scheduled to begin their sleep period for the day at 2:20 a.m., and will receive their wake up call to begin flight day 6 at 10:50 a.m. on Monday, April 6.

Cribbage players welcome!  Here are the rules for engine number cribbage:

Take the numbers on a single locomotive (ie. BNSF 6432) along with the "Cribbage Fifth Number" from the banner.  First add combinations of the numbers together to total 15.  Each combination which totals 15 is two points (6+4+3+2=15)  Sets of the same number then count as two points for each pair.  Examples would be UP 2844 worth two points, UP 4333 worth six points, UP 5555 worth ten points, etc.  Finally a run of three consecutive numbers is worth three points ( example: 4, 3 and 2) and a run of four consecutive numbers is worth four points (example: 5, 4, 3 and 2) and a run of five worth five points.  Add up sets, runs and 15's for a total score on that "hand".  Use the "Cribbage Fifth Number" from the banner to make a hand of five cards.  Enjoy!

ABOUT THIS LIVESTREAM:
Virtual Railfan’s LIVE train cameras capture trains on BNSF’s Chillicothe Sub from the Fort Madison Marina as they cross the Mississippi River via the historic Fort Madison Swing Bridge. Completed in 1927, this mile-long engineering marvel features a 525-foot swing span — the longest of its kind at the time — carrying two railway tracks below and a two-lane roadway above. The bridge, manned 24/7, opens over 2,000 times a year for river traffic, which has priority per US Coast Guard regulations.

The BNSF Chillicothe Sub, part of the Southern Transcon, is a key freight corridor, also hosting Amtrak’s Southwest Chief, which stops daily at the historic Fort Madison Depot. Union Pacific has trackage rights on this line, adding to the traffic mix. Also visible is the BNSF Hannibal Sub, or "K-Line," a single-track route linking Burlington, IA, and St. Louis, MO. Together, both subdivisions see around 65 trains per day.

ABOUT THIS LIVESTREAM:
Virtual Railfan’s LIVE PTZ train camera at Galesburg, Illinois, at Milepost 162.327 on BNSF’s Chicago Division (Ottumwa, Mendota, and Brookfield Subdivisions), is hosted by the Discovery Depot Children’s Museum. Overlooking the modern Galesburg Amtrak Station, the Galesburg Railroad Museum, and its Burlington Route steam locomotive (No. 3006), this camera captures a historic rail hub 45 miles northwest of Peoria. Galesburg hosts BNSF’s third-largest classification yard, built by the Chicago, Burlington & Quincy Railroad (CB&Q) in 1905, handling thousands of freight cars daily.

The camera shows 40+ BNSF freight trains and 8 daily Amtrak trains: California Zephyr, Southwest Chief, Carl Sandburg, and Illinois Zephyr. The Amtrak Station and Railroad Museum, with its steam locomotive, caboose, and baggage car, offers a historic backdrop to the action. Daily train counts hit 80–100, with mid to late afternoon light ideal for photography.

Experience the raw beauty and emotional power of the Pacific Northwest through this LIVE webcam from Pacific City, Oregon – Cape Kiwanda 🌊✨. Here, every crashing wave, every shifting cloud, and every glowing sunset creates a living painting that changes by the minute. This is not just a view — it’s a feeling. A deep breath of ocean air, a moment of calm, a spark of adventure. Whether you’re watching from across the world or just planning your next getaway, this camera connects you instantly to one of the most breathtaking places on the Oregon Coast.

The live stream opens up to the wide, sandy shores of Pacific City Beach, a place where time seems to slow down. In the early morning, soft mist rolls over the water like a dreamy veil 😌🌫️. Seagulls glide effortlessly above the waves, and the gentle rumble of the ocean feels almost therapeutic. As the day unfolds, sunlight brightens the cliffs of Cape Kiwanda, revealing their warm orange-gold tones, sculpted by centuries of wind and water. The beach becomes alive with surfers paddling out through glittering swells, families strolling along the shoreline, and travelers pausing just to take in the view — and maybe, like you, falling a little bit in love with this place 💙.

One of the most iconic sights you'll see on this stream is the majestic Haystack Rock rising from the sea like a silent guardian. Powerful yet peaceful, this massive offshore monolith brings a sense of awe every time you see it. Depending on the season, the water around it shifts from deep blue to emerald green, reflecting moods that range from calm serenity to wild, restless storms. It's impossible not to feel something while watching it — wonder, stillness, or maybe a spark of wanderlust ✨.

The sweeping dunes of Cape Kiwanda add movement and emotion to the landscape. The shapes of the dunes change constantly, as if the wind is painting the sand in slow motion. On sunny days, you may spot travelers climbing the steep dune trail, silhouettes glowing against the sky as they reach the top. From there, they can see the entire curve of the bay — a view the webcam often catches during moments of perfect light. And when the waves are big, surfers gather along this section of coast, chasing adrenaline and joy as they ride the ocean’s energy 🏄‍♂️💛.

This live camera also captures the rhythm of daily life in Pacific City. Local fishermen preparing their dories, visitors laughing as the waves rush over their feet, dogs running happily along the beach — these tiny moments give the stream a warm, human touch. They remind you that Pacific City isn't just a scenic destination, but a living community full of stories and emotion.

Sunsets here are unforgettable. As the sun dips toward the horizon, the sky ignites in shades of orange, pink, purple, and gold 🌅💖. Every evening is a new masterpiece, a moment of magic you can watch in real time. And when night falls, the ocean turns mysterious and calm, with moonlight shimmering on the water and the faint glow of distant stars adding a peaceful, almost spiritual stillness.

Whether you're here to check the surf, to relax, to escape for a moment, or simply to feel the soothing presence of nature, this Cape Kiwanda LIVE webcam is your window to one of the most stunning and emotionally powerful landscapes on the West Coast. From stormy days full of dramatic waves ⚡🌊 to quiet mornings that feel like a deep exhale, this stream lets you connect to the ocean’s soul — anytime, from anywhere.

Take a moment. Breathe. Enjoy the beauty of Oregon’s coast. You’re always welcome here 🌴💙🌊.

description:

Located deep in the heart of Africa in the Sabi and Game Reserve, Nkorho Bush Lodge is home to an incredible diversity of wildlife and is the premier way to experience the African bushveld. The park also shares 50 kilometers of its unfenced boundary line with neighboring Kruger National Park allowing animals pass casually between the two parks, unhindered and abundant.

Utopia Village Sandy Channel Underwater Reef Cam powered by EXPLORE.org
description:
This camera is installed 320 ft from shore at a depth of 20 ft, in a sandy channel leading to the reef wall that drops 120 ft to the ocean floor. Special things to keep a look-out for here include Eagle Rays, Stingrays, Moray Eels, Turtles, Sharks and various kinds of Snappers and Groupers.

Utopia Village is located on the south shore of Utila, the smallest of the Bay Islands, off the northern coast of Honduras in the western Caribbean. It sits on a white sand and coral beach, surrounded by green jungle, turquoise waters and the beautiful Meso-American reef that comes right up to its shore. Before Covid-19 it was the #1-rated dive resort on Utila, but since then it has been refocused to be a permanent home for our staff and their families, as well as a base for local marine research and conservation non-profits.

title: Africam Tau powered by EXPLORE.org
description:
On the Northern border of South Africa and Botswana lies a beautiful area known as the Groot Marico, named after the Marico River. This spectacular setting houses Tau Game Lodge situated in the Madikwe Game Reserve (the 4th largest game reserve in the country). The lodge overlooks Tau Waterhole,  an oasis for animals and wildlife including elephants, giraffes, lions, tigers, zebras, and cheetahs .
Along with these animals this area attracts over 27 species of game and is a prime spot for bird watchers. The waterhole also houses resident nile crocodiles that prey on small game and birds. The Tau live camera gives viewers a secret window into this sanctuary and it’s robust South African ecosystem as elephants bathe, lions roar, wild dogs roam, and crocodiles prey.
